title: AUC-Safe Stacker With Sigmoid Calibration Only
summary: For ROC AUC, preserving ranking matters more than perfect probability calibration.
rationale: For ROC AUC, preserving ranking matters more than perfect probability calibration. Scikit-learn notes sigmoid calibration is strictly monotonic and therefore preserves ranking, while isotonic can introduce ties and change AUC. That makes sigmoid the safer post-processing for this competition.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold predictions from 2-4 diverse models, fit a very small logistic stacker or Platt-style sigmoid on OOF scores only, and apply the learned monotonic combiner to test predictions. Prefer this over isotonic unless leaderboard evidence clearly favors ties.
expected_effect: Improves blend reliability and leaderboard transfer without sacrificing the ranking signal that drives the metric.
risk: A stacker trained on leaky OOF predictions or same-race mixed folds will overstate gains; isotonic may still help log loss but hurt AUC via ties.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
